summary: Camera-system decompilation PR that added full C implementations for Camera_8002958C and Camera_8002A768, a partial implementation for Camera_80029CF8, cleanup/refinement in Camera_80029AAC, Camera_8002B0E0, and Camera_800307D0, and corresponding camera header/static type updates. The decomp.dev report credited +3360 matched bytes, with Camera_8002958C and Camera_8002A768 reaching 100% and Camera_80029CF8 improving to 37.19%. Human review text was absent in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented Camera_8002958C, replacing a nonmatching stub marker with logic that counts active CameraBox subjects, applies cm_803BCB9C fixedness and Stage_GetCamTrackRatio, clamps subject positions against camera bounds, handles the zero-subject fallback via Stage_UnkSetVec3TCam_Offset, and writes CameraBounds fields including subjects and z_pos.

Refactored Camera_80029AAC variable names from generic var_f* style into spread, follow_speed, delta, lerp_factor, offset_x, and offset_y, while preserving the interest interpolation behavior and clamping lerp_factor to [0.0001f, 1.0f].

Added a partial Camera_80029CF8 implementation that computes target interest/position from camera bounds, FOV, stage pan/pitch data, aspect ratio, tanf frustum math, zoom limits, and assert checks against M_PI_2.

Reworked Camera_8002A768 from a messy/nonmatching translation into a matching implementation using Vec3 corner rays, half_fov, pitch/pan angles, frustum corner projection, bounds/blast-zone overlap checks, and target_position/target_interest correction.
Improved Camera_800307D0 by introducing a static inline compute_edge helper that uses sinf/cosf, fabs_inline, and edge projection to compute left/right view limits.
Adjusted Camera_8002B0E0 around c-stick/zoom state so x2BC is updated from cm_80452C68.x2BC directly instead of a stale/local x2bc expression shown in the diff.

Updated camera.h prototypes for newly typed functions such as Camera_8002958C(CameraBounds*, CameraTransformState*), Camera_80029CF8(CameraBounds*, CameraTransformState*), and Camera_8002A768(CameraTransformState*, s32), and removed many parameter names from prototypes.
Changed cm_803BCB9C from a CameraFixednessMult struct object to a static f32 array of 5 elements, then removed CameraFixednessMult from forward.h and types.h.
smart_moves: Converted cm_803BCB9C to a plain f32[5], matching the implementation pattern in Camera_8002958C where the active subject count indexes fixedness directly.

Used semantic local names in formerly generic decomp output: spread, follow_speed, lerp_factor, offset_x, offset_y, half_fov, pitch_angle, top_overlap, right_overlap, bottom_overlap, and left_overlap make camera behavior easier to audit without changing symbol names.

Reused vector operations directly with Vec3 variables instead of pointer casts to individual fields, e.g. lbVector_Rotate(&top_left, ...), lbVector_Normalize(&top_left), and lbVector_Add(&top_left, ...).
Factored repeated Camera_800307D0 edge-projection math into compute_edge, reducing duplicated sinf/cosf/threshold logic while still improving match percentage.

Preserved match-sensitive oddities and unresolved spots with comments rather than over-cleaning: PAD_STACK[8] with TODO, empty if bodies, explicit Stage_GetCamBounds* calls whose return values are unused, and assert strings/line numbers in Camera_80029CF8.
Used explicit numeric thresholds and constants visible in the diff, such as -0.001f frustum z checks, 8.5070587e37f sentinel values, 80.0f/5000.0f z scaling, and 17.5f pan clamps.
decomp_lessons: Camera matching may require keeping seemingly redundant calls, empty branches, assert line numbers, and exact floating-point constants because they can affect codegen and match stability.
When a static struct is only used as an indexed table, replacing it with a raw f32 array can simplify access and better reflect the original data layout.

Use domain names for camera math locals where evidence is clear: bounds spread, follow speed, lerp factor, half FOV, pitch/pan angles, overlap corrections, and target interest/position all made this diff more maintainable.
For frustum/camera-bound functions, writing out top-left/top-right/bottom-right/bottom-left corner vectors and applying the same rotate-normalize-project sequence can tame messy m2c output.

Inlining helper functions can be a viable matching tactic if it reduces repeated expression noise while preserving compiler behavior; compute_edge improved Camera_800307D0 according to the bot report.
Header cleanup should track real function signatures once functions are decompiled, but avoid adding parameter names unless they add value or are known.
naming_conventions: Use Camera_800xxxxx for unresolved camera functions, preserving project symbol style.
Prefer CameraBounds*, CameraTransformState*, CameraBox*, HSD_GObj*, and HSD_CObj* typed signatures over UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once function arguments are known.
Use s32 rather than int in camera prototypes when the decomp evidence supports project-width integer style, as done for Camera_8002A768.
Use gobj for HSD_GObj* locals/parameters instead of arg0 when the role is clear.
Use descriptive camera math locals such as spread, follow_speed, lerp_factor, offset_x, offset_y, half_fov, pitch_angle, pan_angle, top_overlap, right_overlap, bottom_overlap, and left_overlap.
Static camera globals retain cm_803B*/cm_804D*/cm_80452* names when not yet semantically named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added u8 PAD_STACK[8] / PAD_STACK(8) in camera functions where stack shape likely mattered.
Kept fabs_inline usage and added platform.h, indicating match-sensitive inline math support.
Used enum_t bitmasks for in_bounds, var_r30, and var_r31 to preserve camera-bound side flags.
Preserved bit flag meanings through expressions like in_bounds & 4, in_bounds & 8, in_bounds & 1, in_bounds & 2 and var_r31 side-overlap checks.
Kept assert calls in Camera_80029CF8 with file name camera.c, line numbers 0x4FA/0x4FB/0x508/0x509, and original FOV messages.
Used deg_to_rad and M_PI_2 rather than hardcoded equivalent constants in newly written camera FOV math.
Factored repeated left/right projection into static inline compute_edge while retaining sentinels ±8.5070587e37f and threshold checks.
Initialized four frustum corner vectors with chained Vec3 assignment from cm_WorldForward, then rotated each corner by signed half_fov combinations.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code for GALE01 improved to 34.72%, +0.09%, +3360 bytes.
New 100% matches: Camera_8002958C (+1312 bytes) and Camera_8002A768 (+692 bytes).
Unmatched-function improvements: Camera_80029CF8 from 0.00% to 37.19% (+359 bytes), Camera_800307D0 from 73.12% to 87.50% (+92 bytes), and Camera_8002B0E0 from 52.86% to 67.14% (+39 bytes).
No human review comments or PR body were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching Camera_80029CF8 to see how the 37.19% partial implementation was completed.
Search for cleanup or renaming of Camera_8002958C locals sp50/sp44/sp30/sp34 after this PR.
Check whether the TODO comments about inline behavior and Camera_8002A768 arg1 always being 0 were resolved in subsequent camera work.
Look for later fixes to camera.h around the 029AAC prototype area, since the diff excerpt still shows an unexpected Camera_80028F5C declaration at that address.
Compare future uses of compute_edge to determine whether this inline helper remained stable for matching Camera_800307D0.
